title: Infosys appoints Nitin Paranjpe as Vice Chairman and approves promoter reclassification request
detailed_summary: Infosys said its Board approved the appointment of Nitin Paranjpe as non-executive Vice Chairman of the Board with immediate effect on April 30, 2026. The document states Nandan Nilekani will continue as Chairman of the Board as long as necessary and that there is no plan for a chairman transition. The Board also approved a request from Mr. Shreyas Shibulal and Ms. Bhairavi Madhusudhan Shibulal to be reclassified from the 'Promoter and Promoter Group' category to the 'Public' category, subject to Regulation 31A requirements, including shareholder approval as may be required. The applicants together held ~ 0.56% of Infosys's equity share capital as on April 30, 2026, and the Board stated the reclassification would not impact Infosys's operations, Board, management, or shareholders. The Board also approved allotment of 35,384 equity shares under the 2015 Incentive Compensation Plan, increasing issued and subscribed share capital to ₹ 20, 27,82,93,815/- divided into 4,05,56,58,763 equity shares of face value of ₹ 5/ - each.

key_facts.fact: The Board approved the appointment of Nitin Paranjpe as Vice Chairman of the Board with immediate effect.
key_facts.section_heading: 1. Appointment of Nitin Paranjpe as Vice Chairman of the Company:

key_facts.fact: Infosys described Nitin Paranjpe's role as non-executive-Vice Chairman of the Board.
Nandan Nilekani stated that he will continue as Chairman of the Board as long as necessary and that there is no plan for a chairman transition.

The Board approved the request from Mr. Shreyas Shibulal and Ms. Bhairavi Madhusudhan Shibulal for reclassification from the 'Promoter and Promoter Group' category to the 'Public' category, subject to Regulation 31A requirements including shareholder approval as may be required.
key_facts.section_heading: 2. Re-classification of Mr. Shreyas Shibulal and Ms. Bhairavi Madhusudhan Shibulal from 'Promoter and Promoter Group' to 'public' category:

key_facts.fact: The applicants together held ~ 0.56% of the equity share capital of the Company as on April 30, 2026.
The Board stated that reclassification of the applicants will not have any impact on the operations of the Company, or on the Board, Management, or Shareholders of the Company.
The Board approved allotment of 35,384 equity shares of face value of ₹ 5 each pursuant to exercise of Restricted Stock Units by eligible employees under the 2015 Incentive Compensation Plan.

corporate_actions.description: The Board approved the allotment of 35,384 equity shares of face value of ₹ 5 each, pursuant to the exercise of Restricted Stock Units by eligible employees under the 2015 Incentive Compensation Plan.
corporate_actions.amount_or_ratio: 35,384 equity shares of face value of ₹ 5 each

corporate_actions.description: Effective from April 30, 2026, the issued and subscribed share capital of the Company stands increased to ₹ 20, 27,82,93,815/- divided into 4,05,56,58,763 equity shares of face value of ₹ 5/ - each.
corporate_actions.amount_or_ratio: ₹ 20, 27,82,93,815/- divided into 4,05,56,58,763 equity shares of face value of ₹ 5/ - each

sample_questions: What leadership change did Infosys announce on April 30, 2026?
Was Nitin Paranjpe appointed as Infosys Chairman or Vice Chairman?
Did Infosys indicate any plan for a chairman transition after appointing Nitin Paranjpe as Vice Chairman?
Who requested reclassification from Infosys's promoter group to public category?
What percentage of Infosys equity did Shreyas Shibulal and Bhairavi Madhusudhan Shibulal hold together as on April 30, 2026?
What conditions apply to Infosys's approval of the Shibulal promoter reclassification request?
How many shares did Infosys allot under the 2015 Incentive Compensation Plan on April 30, 2026?
What was Infosys's issued and subscribed share capital after the April 30, 2026 share allotment?
processing_notes: Some text in the embedded request letter is visibly corrupted or missing spacing; extraction relied on clear readable passages and avoided corrupted clauses where meaning was incomplete. Generic company profile, safe harbor language, exchange-recipient details, signatures, addresses, and media contacts were excluded.
